mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 3.8%, tight labor market conditions signal wage-push inflation and potential Fed tightening, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the threshold crossing shows no predictive power for forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in industrial production and housing starts indicates broad-based economic acceleration that outpaces monetary accommodation, leading to valuation compression and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or out-of-sample backtests show no significant alpha after transaction costs.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rising inflation expectations, an increase in the federal funds rate accelerates discount rate growth faster than earnings revisions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline significantly when both proxies rise concurrently, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression over 24 months.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP expansion signals improving corporate earnings fundamentals, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as valuation multiples stabilize and risk premiums contract.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no positive correlation with 60-day forward equity returns, or the predictive relationship reverses during recessionary periods without regime switching.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 for two consecutive months, robust hiring validates economic resilience, reducing equity risk premiums and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k in two consecutive months
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of occurrences, or the threshold crossing fails to improve out-of-sample Sharpe ratios over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates sectoral rotation from rate-sensitive real estate to cyclical manufacturing, which temporarily boosts equity momentum and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal shows no predictive power for short-term equity returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-funds-rate environment, a sudden spike in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand that overrides inflation concerns, prompting Fed pivot expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 1.5% and UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: Equity returns do not rise following the specified labor deterioration under tight policy, or the forward return distribution shows no significant shift in mean or variance over a 2-year walk-forward test.
